Table of Contentsinnova Brand Guidelines
Define
Our Brand
Why innova exists03

Work has changed. But the way it is built has not.

Organisations have evolved how they work, where they work, and who they work with. Yet the systems that support work are still treated as separate decisions. Space is planned in isolation. Technology is layered on after. People adapt to what already exists.

innova exists to remove that friction.

We are an integrated enterprise partner. We bring together the three essentials of work and make them function as one connected system. Environments designed. Teams enabled. Technology integrated. One partner. One relationship.

Our mission

To be the first name an enterprise thinks of when it needs to set up, staff, and equip a workplace in India.

Why innova existsinnova Brand Guidelines
What we believe04
Our belief
Work works best when
everything works together.

The workspace shapes collaboration. The people determine whether technology gets used. The technology defines whether the workspace performs. These are not parallel tracks. They are a single system.

Our promise
Everything work needs. One partner.
Space to work in. People to work with. Technology to power it.
What we believeinnova Brand Guidelines
What we are. What we are not.05

Clarity comes from boundaries. These contrasts define where innova operates and where it does not.

✓ innova is✕ innova is not
Integrated. One partner across space, people, and technology.A collection of disconnected services sharing a name.
Enterprise-focused. Built for organisations that treat workplace as infrastructure.A generic provider trying to serve everyone.
Outcome-driven. Measured by whether the workplace performs.Task-driven. Filling seats, racking hardware, processing requisitions.
Warm and confident. Three decades of experience, expressed directly.Cold and corporate. Jargon designed to impress rather than communicate.
Premium with personality. Enterprise rigour paired with genuine warmth.A co-working or lifestyle brand.
Specific. Real clients, real numbers, real outcomes.Vague. Buzzwords and stock photography instead of proof.
A growth partner. Invested in the long-term relationship.A vendor. Transactional and replaceable.
What we are. What we are not.innova Brand Guidelines
The three verticals06

One company. Three capabilities. Each vertical has its own market and clients. What connects them is the innova standard: enterprise-grade, managed end to end, under one relationship.

innova Space
The room where it happens.
Managed offices and coworking across Noida NCR. From five-seat flex desks to full-floor managed facilities. Design-led environments that work as hard as the teams inside them.
6 NCR locations · Gruner: 5→197 seats · Sector 142 opening 2026
innova People
The talent that makes it work.
IT staffing, recruitment, and workforce solutions. Backed by JobMojo, a proprietary AI recruitment platform. Enterprise-scale talent acquisition with the speed and precision of a specialist.
Capgemini, Genpact · JobMojo AI tool · 3.5L LinkedIn followers
innova Tech
The technology that powers it.
AV integration, smart meeting rooms, and digital workspace technology. Jabra Connect JV partner in India. The infrastructure layer that makes collaboration seamless across any workspace.
Jabra Connect JV · Honda, Honeywell, HCL, Optum
The three verticalsinnova Brand Guidelines
Brand architecture07

Branded House. One master brand. Three vertical descriptors.

Every piece of marketing builds one name. The architecture was chosen because brand recall is innova's primary growth lever. Consolidating under one name turns every touchpoint into cross-visibility.

Master brand is the default for corporate communications, partnerships, and the integration story.

Vertical identifiers are used when a specific capability competes in its own market. The vertical name always includes the master brand.

Naming convention: Always "innova" + vertical name. Never abbreviate to just "Space" or "People" or "Tech" without the master brand prefix. The vertical name is a descriptor, not a sub-brand.

innova
Master Brand
innova Space
Managed Offices
innova People
Talent Solutions
innova Tech
AV Integration
Brand architectureinnova Brand Guidelines

Positioning statement

India's Integrated Workplace Partner.

Marketing tagline
Everything work needs. One partner.
Hoardings, website hero, social headers, brand films.
Enterprise pitch
Workspace design, talent solutions, and technology infrastructure. One relationship.
Sales meetings, tenders, investor communications.
Vertical promise
Space to work in. People to work with. Technology to power it.
Brand storytelling, carousels, internal communications.
Anniversary
Built over three decades. Designed for the next.
2026 launch period only. Retire after anniversary year.

Tone of voice09

The composed senior partner. Warm but never casual. Precise but never cold.

Traditional
Progressive
Exclusive
Accessible
Understated
Bold
Serious
Playful
Write this
"One partner instead of three. Your space designed, your team placed, your technology running."
Not this
"Leveraging our holistic approach to seamlessly integrate workplace solutions."
Confident
not arrogant
States what innova does without hedging. Three decades of work earns the right to speak directly.
Warm
not casual
Personality in how innova communicates. Short sentences that land. Never slang or forced friendliness.
Clear
not simplistic
Explains complex things without dumbing them down. If a sentence can be shorter, it should be.
Grounded
not boastful
Every claim backed by evidence. Real clients, real numbers, real outcomes.
Tone of voiceinnova Brand Guidelines
Design
Elements
The wordmark11

Lowercase. Inktrap. No period.

The innova wordmark is set in PP Neue Machina Inktrap UltraBold, a typeface whose ink traps give the letterforms structural detail at every scale. It is always placed as an outlined SVG vector, never typed live.

Key construction details:

The i tittle is a perfect circle, matching the geometry of the brandmark. The v junction carries a slight optical thickening to maintain even colour at small sizes. All terminals are squared, consistent with the typeface's industrial character.

Always lowercase. The name is never capitalised, never set in all caps. The lowercase form is the identity.

No period. Earlier materials included a period after the wordmark. This has been permanently removed.

PP Neue Machina Inktrap UltraBold · Always outlined SVG
The wordmarkinnova Brand Guidelines
The wordmark12

Clear space

The n-height system governs clear space. Measure the height of the lowercase "n" in the wordmark. That dimension becomes the minimum exclusion zone on all four sides. No other element, text, or edge may enter this zone.

Minimum reproduction sizes

ContextMinimum width
Print25mm
Digital80px

Trademark

The TM symbol appears on formal identity contexts only: brand guidelines, letterhead, website first instance, presentation title slides. Positioned as open superscript after the final "a" at approximately 35% of cap height. Omit on social media, signage fabrication, and merchandise.

Colour versions

Charcoal on WhiteIvory on CharcoalCharcoal on IvoryWhite on Cinnamon
The wordmarkinnova Brand Guidelines
The brandmark13

The i in squircle. Standalone identity at any scale.

The brandmark extracts the "i" letterform from the wordmark and places it inside a squircle container. The squircle radius matches the rounded rectangle proportions of the wordmark's ink traps, creating geometric continuity between the two marks.

Construction: The i tittle is a perfect circle whose diameter matches the stem width. The stem is centred vertically and horizontally within the container. The squircle corner radius is 16.4% of the container width.

Versions

The filled version is the primary mark. Outlined, reversed, and monochrome versions exist for specific contexts. The filled brandmark on dark backgrounds uses Ivory fill. On light backgrounds, Charcoal.

Clear space

50% of the brandmark width on all sides. More generous than the wordmark because the brandmark often appears in tighter contexts.

ContextMinimum size
Print8mm
Digital24px
Filled primary · Charcoal on white
The brandmarkinnova Brand Guidelines
The brandmark14

When to use the brandmark

ContextMark
App iconBrandmark
Social media avatarBrandmark
FaviconBrandmark
Co-branding partner lockupBrandmark
Environmental signage where wordmark appears nearbyBrandmark
Watermark or background patternBrandmark outline

Rule: The brandmark never replaces the wordmark as the primary identity in any branded material. It supplements. The wordmark is always the first choice. The brandmark is for contexts where the wordmark cannot function at scale or where the full name is already established nearby.

Filled on WhiteFilled on CharcoalWhite on TwilightWhite on Hunter
The brandmarkinnova Brand Guidelines
The descriptor15

SPACE • PEOPLE • TECH

The descriptor communicates the three-vertical structure in a single line. It sits beneath the wordmark, separated by one n-height of vertical space. It never appears without the wordmark above it.

Formatting

TypefaceDM Sans Bold
CaseAll capitals
Tracking+300
SeparatorsBullet • Unicode U+2022
Colour on lightStone #6B6B6B
Colour on darkSilver #A0A0A0

Alignment

Centred beneath the wordmark. Baseline of the descriptor sits exactly one n-height below the wordmark baseline. This creates optical breathing room without arbitrary spacing.

When to include

Website header, business cards, letterhead, signage, presentation title slides, brand guidelines.

Full lockup with descriptor · Charcoal on white
The descriptorinnova Brand Guidelines
Logo usage rules16

Three tiers. One rule: always build the master brand.

Tier 1 · Primary
Wordmark
Default for all branded materials. Corporate communications, website, social headers, presentations, signage. If you can only use one mark, use this.
Tier 2 · Standalone
Brandmark
App icons, social avatars, favicons, co-branding, environmental contexts where the wordmark appears elsewhere. Supplements but never replaces.
Tier 3 · Co-branding
Combined lockup
Brandmark + wordmark + descriptor. Reserved for co-branding lockups, joint ventures, and formal identity contexts only. Never as the everyday mark.

The default is Tier 1. If there is any doubt about which mark to use, the wordmark alone is always correct. Every use of the brandmark or combined lockup should have a specific reason.

Logo usage rulesinnova Brand Guidelines
Logo usage rules17

These restrictions apply to the wordmark, brandmark, and all lockups. No exceptions.

Do not stretch, skew, or distort
Do not render in a vertical colour
Do not reduce opacity below 40%
Innova
Do not use title case or all caps
Do not rotate the wordmark
Do not add shadows or effects
innova.
Do not add a period after innova
innova
Do not recreate in another typeface
Do not place on busy backgrounds
Do not outline the wordmark
innova®
Do not use ® or © symbols
i
Do not change the squircle to a circle
Logo usage rulesinnova Brand Guidelines
Logo usage rules18

Background applications

The wordmark exists in two colour modes: Charcoal for light backgrounds, Ivory or White for dark backgrounds. On vertical-coloured backgrounds, always use White.

White
Ivory
Charcoal
Cinnamon
Twilight
Hunter
Photography

If in doubt, use the Charcoal wordmark on a white background. It is always correct.

Logo usage rulesinnova Brand Guidelines
Colour palette19

Three verticals. One neutral system. The 80/20 rule.

Neutrals carry 80% of the visual weight in any innova layout. Vertical colours provide 20% accent. This ratio is non-negotiable. It keeps the brand feeling premium and prevents the three colours from competing.

Cinnamon
#D4772E
RGB 212, 119, 46 · CMYK 0, 44, 78, 17
Pantone 7571 C · innova Space
Twilight Indigo
#2D4272
RGB 45, 66, 114 · CMYK 61, 42, 0, 55
Pantone 534 C · innova People
Hunter Green
#3D6B4F
RGB 61, 107, 79 · CMYK 43, 0, 26, 58
Pantone 7734 C · innova Tech

Constraint: No red or red-adjacent shades. Wine, berry, vermillion, and burgundy are permanently excluded from the palette. Coral is acceptable only within the Cinnamon family.

Colour paletteinnova Brand Guidelines

Neutral scale

Seven-step neutral scale with warm undertone. These are the structural colours of every innova layout. Never use pure #000000.

Colour usage + accessibility21

WCAG AA minimum. AAA where possible.

Every text-on-background pairing must meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for body text and 3:1 for large text.

Aa
Charcoal on White
16.8:1 · AAA
Aa
Ivory on Charcoal
15.9:1 · AAA
Aa
Graphite on Ivory
9.3:1 · AAA
Aa
White on Cinnamon
3.4:1 · AA Large
Aa
White on Twilight
7.2:1 · AAA
Aa
White on Hunter
4.9:1 · AA
Do
Use Charcoal text on light backgrounds. Use White or Ivory text on dark and vertical-coloured backgrounds. Use vertical colours as accent borders, fills, and highlights, not as text colour on white.
Do not
Use Stone text on Ivory. Use Cinnamon text on white for body copy. Place vertical colours adjacent to each other as equal-weight blocks. Let any single vertical colour exceed 20% of a layout.
Colour usage + accessibilityinnova Brand Guidelines

Data, labels, captions, metadata

The wordmark typeface is PP Neue Machina Inktrap UltraBold from Pangram Pangram foundry. It is used exclusively for the wordmark, never for running text. It is not available in Canva. All wordmark instances are placed as outlined SVG vectors.

Typographyinnova Brand Guidelines

Digital type scale

LevelTypefaceWeightSizeLeadingUse
H1Fraunces40048px56pxPage hero, one per page
H2Fraunces40032px40pxSection headers

LevelTypefaceWeightSizeLeadingUse
DisplayFraunces40036pt42ptCover titles, pull quotes
SectionFraunces50024pt28ptSection openers
HeadingDM Sans60014pt18ptPage headings
BodyDM Sans40010pt14ptRunning text
CaptionSpace Mono4007pt10ptLabels, legal, footnotes

Context-specific sizes

Business cards: name at 10pt DM Sans 600, title at 8pt DM Sans 400, contact at 7pt Space Mono 400. Signage: reception wordmark minimum 150mm width, room labels minimum 80mm wordmark width. The rule: if you cannot read it at arm's length in print, it is too small.

Typography scaleinnova Brand Guidelines
Visual
Language
Illustration: The Ink Register25

Two layers. Ink first. Colour second.

The Ink Register is a proprietary illustration system built for innova. It operates like offset printing: a black ink plate provides the complete drawing, and a separate colour register adds flat accent fills to 15-30% of the image area.

The ink plate functions as a finished illustration on its own. Colour enhances but never replaces it. This separation means any illustration can work in pure black, or with one, two, or three brand colours applied.

Lineage

The style sits between three editorial illustrators: the gestural confidence of Ilya Milstein, the conceptual clarity of Christoph Niemann, and the warm humanity of Timo Kuilder. The result is editorial, not corporate. Gestural, not geometric. Warm, not clinical.

Line quality

Confident brushpen linework with variable weight. Heavier on outer contours, lighter on interior details. Lines are organic with occasional breaks where strokes trail off. The feeling of a Pentel Pocket Brush Pen on smooth paper.

Illustration: The Ink Registerinnova Brand Guidelines
Illustration: The Ink Register26

Four ink modes control how colour enters an illustration. Mode selection depends on context and vertical.

Mode 0
Pure Ink
All black ink on white. No colour. The most versatile mode. Works at any size, any context. Default for formal and corporate materials.
Mode 1
Single Register
Black ink base with one vertical accent colour on maximum 20% of the image. Colour appears on only one category: screens, one wall plane, or one clothing item. Never more than one.
Mode 2
Double Register
Black ink base with two accent colours. Primary at 15% area, secondary at 10%. The two colours occupy separate visual planes and never touch or overlap.
Mode 3
Tinted Monochrome
No black ink. The entire illustration is rendered in a single brand colour. Linework at full saturation, fills in 20% tint. Creates a duotone effect for vertical-specific materials.
Illustration: The Ink Registerinnova Brand Guidelines
Illustration: The Ink Register27

Drawing people

Hair is the primary identifier. Every figure's hair must be visually distinct: wavy strokes, solid black mass, bun, curls, short crop. Faces use simple dot eyes, small line nose, optional mouth. Hands are 3-4 finger gestural shorthand. Body proportions at approximately 1:5.5 head-to-body ratio.

What gets colour

Technology screens, one architectural plane per scene, or one article of clothing on the primary figure. Skin tones get flat warm fill when faces are close-up and prominent.

What never gets colour

Furniture frames, floor planes, plants, hair, small objects. These remain black line on white, always.

When to use illustration

Social mediaInk Register with vertical colour
Brand storytellingFull Register or Pure Ink
Internal communicationsPure Ink or Single Register
Wayfinding and signagePure Ink at reduced scale

Formal proposals, investor communications, tenders. Photography takes over in contexts where credibility depends on proof rather than personality.

Illustration: The Ink Registerinnova Brand Guidelines
Photography: The Working Notebook28

Kodak Portra 400. Golden hour. Kinfolk quality.

Every innova photograph should feel like it was shot on film during golden hour in a space designed by someone with excellent taste. Warm shadows, creamy highlights, muted greens, organic grain. The benchmark is editorial warmth, not corporate documentation.

Five shot types

Establishing
Wide environmental. The space itself. 28-40mm, f/4-5.6, slightly elevated. 35% quiet zone for annotation overlay.
Action
People working, collaborating, mid-conversation. 50-85mm, f/2.8-3.5, eye level. Candid. Nobody aware of camera.
Detail / Surface
Hands, materials, textures. 85-135mm, f/2-3.5. One plane sharp, everything else dissolves. Fill frame with warmth.
Portrait
Environmental portraits. Subject in their workspace. 85-135mm, f/3.5-4.5. Background readable but soft.
Convergence
Space + people + technology in one frame. Shot through glass or foreground elements. 35-50mm, f/4. Three depth layers.
Photography: The Working Notebookinnova Brand Guidelines
Photography: The Working Notebook29

Colour grading

Target: Kodak Portra 400, golden hour, printed on warm cream paper. Black point lifted to warm brown-black. Highlights drift to ivory-cream. Global saturation reduced 12-15%, with selective boost on brand-colour objects. Fine monochromatic grain. This grade makes photography and the Ink Register feel like they exist on the same printed surface.

Colour temperature

3800-4200K. Golden-warm, not amber. Natural window light as primary source, always. Side light preferred. Dappled light through plant leaves is a signature element.

Dual-layer compositing

Photography is the reality layer. The Ink Register is the annotation layer. Every photograph accommodates illustration overlay: quiet zones, depth-of-field softness, visual calm in specific areas. The relationship is a printed page someone has drawn on with a good pen.

Ink line opacity stays at 100% on photographs. Colour fills at 85-90% opacity so photo texture reads through. Never overlay on faces, active hands, or screens with real content.

Photography: The Working Notebookinnova Brand Guidelines
Icons30

Derived from the i-dot. Circular logic across the system.

The icon system takes its foundational geometry from the brandmark. The perfect circle of the i tittle becomes the base unit: every icon starts from a circle and builds outward using consistent stroke weights and rounded terminals.

Construction rules

Stroke weight matches the Ink Register line weight at equivalent scale. Corners use the same radius as the squircle container corners, scaled proportionally. Icons sit on a 24px grid for digital, 6mm grid for print. The optical centre of every icon aligns with the mathematical centre of the i-dot.

Style

Outline style, single weight, rounded caps and joins. Not filled. Not dual-tone. The icons share the editorial quality of the Ink Register without competing with it. When Ink Register illustrations are present, icons reduce to their simplest form.

Colour

Icons follow the same colour rules as the wordmark: Charcoal on light backgrounds, Ivory or White on dark. Vertical colours may be used for vertical-specific icon sets in wayfinding and navigation.

Iconsinnova Brand Guidelines
Layout and grid31

The Trap Grid. Geometry from the brandmark.

The layout system derives its proportions from the brandmark's squircle geometry. The corner radius ratio of 16.4% translates to margin and gutter proportions across all formats. This creates visual consistency between the identity mark and every page, screen, and surface it appears on.

Spacing system

Base unit: 4mm print, 8px digital. All spacing values are multiples of this unit. Margins, gutters, padding, and component spacing align to this grid. The system creates rhythm without rigidity.

Column structure

Desktop web12 columns, 24px gutter
Tablet8 columns, 16px gutter
Mobile4 columns, 16px gutter
Print A46 columns, 5mm gutter
Presentation 16:912 columns, 20px gutter

The quiet zone principle

Every layout reserves at least 30% as breathing space. This mirrors the photography quiet zone requirement and the Ink Register's 40-60% white space rule. Density is the enemy of the innova aesthetic. If a layout feels crowded, remove content before reducing spacing.

Layout and gridinnova Brand Guidelines
Motion principles32

Slow confidence. Never frantic. Never static.

Motion in the innova system follows the brand personality: deliberate, warm, and unhurried. Every animation should feel like a considered gesture, not a flourish. The brand moves the way an experienced professional speaks: with pace, clarity, and the occasional well-timed pause.

Easing

All transitions use cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) as the default ease. This produces a fast entry with a long, soft deceleration. It feels purposeful without being abrupt. Never use linear easing or default browser ease-in-out.

Duration scale

Micro (button states, toggles)120-180ms
Standard (cards, panels, reveals)300-400ms
Emphasis (page transitions, hero reveals)600-800ms
Dramatic (logo reveal, launch sequence)1000-1400ms

Logo reveal sequence

The brandmark fades in at 0ms (400ms duration). The wordmark draws left-to-right starting at 300ms (800ms duration). The descriptor fades up from 10px below at 900ms (400ms duration). Stagger creates the reading rhythm: mark, name, role.

Transition style

Prefer opacity + translateY over scale transforms. Elements enter from below (translateY: 20px to 0) with opacity fade. Slide direction is always upward for new content, reflecting forward momentum. Horizontal slides reserved for carousels and tab switching only.

Motion principlesinnova Brand Guidelines
Pattern and texture33

Three textures. Each tied to identity geometry.

The pattern library gives designers repeating elements that reinforce the brand without competing with the Ink Register or photography. Every pattern derives directly from the identity system. No decorative additions.

Squircle grid

A repeating tile of the brandmark squircle at 20% opacity, rotated 0 degrees, tiled at 2x the brandmark width spacing. Used as background texture on internal documents, presentation slide backgrounds, and environmental wall treatments. Never over photography.

Annotation lines

Thin horizontal rules (0.5px, Mist or Silver) with occasional vertical ticks, mimicking the Ink Register annotation style. Used as section dividers, table backgrounds, and form field treatments. Spacing follows the 4mm/8px grid.

Ink wash texture

A scanned ink wash with grain, reduced to 5-8% opacity, applied as a full-bleed background. Creates the tactile quality of printed paper without obscuring content. Used on feature pages, section openers, and environmental wall panels.

Application rules

Maximum pattern opacity20% on white, 15% on Ivory
Never combineTwo patterns on the same surface
Never place overPhotography, Ink Register, or text
Vertical colour tintingPermitted at 8% opacity maximum
SQUIRCLE GRID
ANNOTATION LINES
INK WASH TEXTURE
Pattern and textureinnova Brand Guidelines
Brand in
Practice
Environmental branding35

The brand on walls, glass, and wayfinding.

Environmental branding translates the identity system into physical space. The annotation concept, central to the Ink Register, becomes literal: the brand marks up the environment the way a designer marks up a drawing.

Reception

Wordmark centred on the wall behind reception, at eye level from seated position. Minimum 150mm width. Material: brushed aluminium or matte black acrylic, pin-mounted with 10mm standoff for shadow depth. Brandmark may appear as a secondary element on the reception desk surface or as a glass etch on the entrance door.

Wall signage

Wordmark at 1500mm from floor to baseline. This height ensures readability from both standing and seated positions. Material: same as reception, or vinyl for glass applications in matte finish.

Room labels

Brandmark at 12mm + room name in DM Sans Bold + capacity in Space Mono. Vertical colour accent as a 2mm bar at the left edge, keyed to the floor or zone vertical. Door-adjacent mounting at 1400mm centre height.

Environmental brandinginnova Brand Guidelines
Environmental branding36

Wayfinding

Directional signage uses icons derived from the brandmark geometry. Charcoal on white or Ivory surfaces. Vertical colour coding by floor or zone: Cinnamon for Space floors, Twilight for People areas, Hunter for Tech zones. An annotation line in the zone colour runs along corridors at 1200mm height.

Ink Register on walls

Large-scale Ink Register illustrations on feature walls in common areas. Pure Ink mode for longevity. Figures at 1.2-1.5x life scale. Application: vinyl print on painted wall or direct UV print. Subject matter should reflect the zone activity.

Material specification

Reception wordmarkBrushed aluminium or matte black acrylic, 3mm depth, pin-mounted 10mm standoff
Room labelsAnodised aluminium plate, 150x100mm, printed or etched
Glass applicationsFrosted vinyl, matte finish, applied interior surface
Wall illustrationsVinyl print on painted wall, matte laminate, or direct UV print
WayfindingAluminium composite panel with vinyl, or wall-mounted acrylic
Environmental brandinginnova Brand Guidelines
Environmental signage37

Every touchpoint speaks the brand. Even the small ones.

Operational signage follows the same design principles as primary environmental branding. The brandmark, colour system, and typography rules apply at every scale. These items are often the most-seen brand elements in a working day.

Meeting room booking plates

150 x 100mm anodised aluminium. Brandmark at 12mm top-left. Room name in DM Sans Bold 14pt. Capacity in Space Mono 10pt below. Left-edge colour bar (2mm width) keyed to floor vertical. Status indicator: green dot for available, Cinnamon dot for occupied.

Danglers and ceiling signs

Double-sided print on 3mm PVC or acrylic. Wordmark on Side A, directional information on Side B. DM Sans Bold for primary text, Space Mono for secondary. Vertical colour accent on the top edge. Minimum wordmark width: 80mm.

Helpdesk and reception signage

Freestanding acrylic or wall-mounted. Brandmark at 20mm minimum. Service title in DM Sans Bold 16pt. Operating hours in Space Mono 9pt. QR code for digital support link positioned bottom-right. White or Ivory background only.

Environmental signageinnova Brand Guidelines
Materials and finishes38

Material choices carry the brand as much as colour and type.

The innova material palette prioritises warmth, tactility, and longevity. Every material should age well. Nothing glossy. Nothing that looks cheap after six months of daily use.

Primary signage
Brushed aluminium
3mm depth, pin-mounted with 10mm standoff. Natural silver finish or anodised black. Creates shadow depth on white walls. Used for reception wordmarks, directional signs, room labels.
Glass applications
Frosted vinyl
Matte finish, applied to interior glass surface. Frost band at 900-1500mm from floor for privacy. Wordmark cut from frost (clear reveal). Safety markings integrated at 1050mm and 1500mm per building code.
Wall treatments
Vinyl print or UV direct
For Ink Register murals and large-scale graphics. Matte laminate over vinyl print. UV direct print on painted walls for permanent installations. Wall must be primed to RAL 9003 Signal White or equivalent. Minimum 150 DPI at final size.
Freestanding elements
Matte black acrylic
5mm thickness for desk signs, 8mm for floor-standing. Laser-cut or CNC-routed. Edges polished to satin. White or Ivory vinyl lettering. Used for reception desk elements, table numbers, and event signage.
Stickers and labels
Matte coated paper or vinyl
Die-cut to squircle shape for brand stickers. Rectangular for asset labels and equipment tags. Always matte finish. Brandmark at minimum 8mm. Colour: Charcoal on white, or white on vertical colour. QR-coded asset tags in Space Mono 7pt.
Excluded materials
What to avoid
No glossy laminates. No chrome or mirror finishes. No foam board for permanent installations. No backlit signage with fluorescent or cool-white LEDs. If backlighting is required, use 3000K warm white only. No plastic standoffs visible from front.
Materials and finishesinnova Brand Guidelines

Presentation templatesinnova Brand Guidelines
Co-branding guidelines43

When the innova brand shares a stage.

Co-branding situations arise in joint ventures, technology partnerships, and client-facing materials where innova appears alongside another identity. The rules protect brand equity while showing respect to the partner.

Lockup structure

The innova brandmark sits left. A vertical divider line (0.5px, Silver) separates the marks. The partner logo sits right. Both marks are optically centred to the same baseline. The divider height matches the smaller mark's height.

Size relationship

The innova brandmark and partner logo should appear at equivalent optical weight. If the partner logo is heavier, reduce it slightly. If lighter, increase it. The goal is visual equality, not mathematical equality.

Clear space in lockup

One brandmark width of clear space between each mark and the divider. The combined lockup requires 1.5x the standard clear space on all external sides.

Technology partnerships

For certified technology partnerships, the innova brandmark may appear in the partner's brand colour as a single flat fill. The partner logo appears in its own brand colours. A "Technology Partner" or "Powered by" descriptor in Space Mono 7pt sits below the lockup.

Restrictions

The wordmark never appears in a partner's typeface. The squircle is never modified for a partner. Vertical colours are never replaced. If the partner must dominate, innova appears as a "Powered by" footer only.

Partner
Logo
EQUAL OPTICAL WEIGHT · DIVIDER AT SMALLER MARK HEIGHT · 1x BRANDMARK WIDTH SPACING
Partner
Logo
Co-branding guidelinesinnova Brand Guidelines
Quick reference44

The rules that matter most. One page.

Identity
Wordmark: Always lowercase "innova" in PP Neue Machina Inktrap UltraBold. Placed as SVG vectors, never typed.
Brandmark: Filled version is primary. Squircle and i are inseparable. No outline variants.
Descriptor: SPACE • PEOPLE • TECH. Bullet separators. Only beneath wordmark.
Default mark: If in doubt, wordmark alone on white. Always correct.
Colour
Ratio: 80% neutrals, 20% vertical colour. Non-negotiable.
Verticals: Cinnamon #D4772E (Space), Twilight #2D4272 (People), Hunter #3D6B4F (Tech).
Neutrals: Charcoal #191919 (never pure black), Ivory #FAF7F3, White #FFF.
Excluded: No red or red-adjacent shades. Permanently.
Typography
Display: Fraunces. Headlines, pull quotes, section titles only.
Body: DM Sans 10pt/14pt. Running text, UI, navigation.
Functional: Space Mono. Labels, data, captions, metadata.
Wordmark font: PP Neue Machina Inktrap UltraBold. Wordmark only. Never for text.
Visual language
Illustration: The Ink Register. Black ink plate first, colour fills 15-30%. Editorial, not corporate.
Photography: The Working Notebook. Kodak Portra 400 grade. 40% quiet zone minimum.
Layout: 30% breathing space minimum. Density is the enemy.
Tone: Confident, warm, clear, grounded. Never cold corporate. Never startup casual.

Anniversary frame: "Built over three decades. Designed for the next."  •  Tagline: "Everything work needs. One partner."  •  Architecture: Branded House

Quick referenceinnova Brand Guidelines
